evidence: We are expanding AI capabilities of our key solutions through a combination of in-house development and partnership with industry leaders, with a focus on agentic capabilities and the development of select use cases in collaboration with clients.
We have made, and continue to make, investments in modern platforms, advanced technologies, open APIs, machine learning and AI, and regulatory technology to support our Capital Markets clients.
enhancing AI capabilities and readiness, and selectively pursuing outsourcing opportunities in technology and operations to support resiliency, agility and cost control.

Our systems use a combination of advanced authentication procedures, predictive analytics, AI modeling and proprietary and shared databases to assess and detect fraud risk for deposit, card and other transactions.

We are expanding AI capabilities of our key solutions through a combination of in-house development and partnership with industry leaders, with a focus on agentic capabilities and the development of select use cases in collaboration with clients.

FIS harnesses advanced analytics, AI, and real-time data insights across our platforms to deliver differentiated solutions that enhance decision-making, improve operational efficiency, and create personalized client experiences.

AI algorithms may produce unfair, unintended, inaccurate, biased or discriminatory outcomes which could be difficult to detect or explain... integration of AI introduces a variety of risks and uncertainties that may harm our reputation, expose us to lawsuits.

The E.U. AI Act imposes a number of requirements... that differ depending on the type and use of a particular AI system, but which will at a minimum include extensive documentation and transparency requirements.
If we do not maintain an adequate model governance function, we may face regulatory risk, lawsuits, security vulnerabilities or other sources of liability and potentially diminish trust in our brand.

limitations: No quantified dollar amounts or headcount figures tied specifically to AI investment were found in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 3 rather than 4 or 5.
AI use cases (fraud detection, agentic capabilities) are named but deployment metrics or quantified business outcomes are absent, capping narrative concreteness at 3.

Risk disclosure is detailed and company-specific with named mechanisms (model governance, EU AI Act compliance timelines, IP/confidentiality risks from AI), but no quantified risk exposure or named internal oversight body is cited, capping at 4.
Promotional language is present in competitive-strengths and strategy sections; evidence from those sections treated as claims rather than confirmed outcomes.
